farfalla.text: The double l flutters; every Italian double is heard — a butterfly.

ombrello.text: Double l, clearly pronounced — an umbrella.

coniglio.text: gli is one liquid sound, not g-l-i — a rabbit.

specchio.text: cch keeps the hard k before i — a mirror.

pipistrello.text: A bat — five syllables of pure Italian rhythm, ending in a double l.

ippopotamo.text: Greek 'river horse'; the double p is heard — a hippopotamus.

cucchiaio.text: cch hard-k plus i-a-i-o — a spoon.

giraffa.text: Soft gi and a double f — a giraffe.

sciarpa.text: sci spells the /sh/ sound — a scarf.

cipolla.text: Double l, always pronounced — an onion.
